diagrams.title: Scatter Plot with Regression Line
diagrams.description: A 2D scatter plot showing training data points (xi, yi) with a straight line representing the hypothesis function h(x) = θ₀ + θ₁x. The vertical distances from each point to the line represent the residuals (errors). This visualizes the core concept of fitting a line to data.
elements: Colored scatter points representing training examples
Straight regression line in contrasting color
Legend or labels for hypothesis equation
diagrams.purpose: Explains the basic concept of linear regression - finding the best-fit line that minimizes the distance between predicted and actual values

diagrams.title: Cost Function Contour Plot
diagrams.description: A contour plot showing the cost function J(θ₀, θ₁) as a 3D bowl-shaped surface viewed from above. Different contour lines represent equal cost values. The minimum (global optimum) is at the center of the innermost circle. This helps visualize how gradient descent navigates to find optimal parameters.
elements: Color gradient from outer (high cost) to inner (low cost)
Optional: path showing gradient descent progression
diagrams.purpose: Illustrates the cost function landscape and how different parameter combinations produce different error values, setting up understanding of optimization

diagrams.title: Gradient Descent Iterations
diagrams.description: A step-by-step visualization showing the gradient descent algorithm converging toward the optimal parameters. Starting from an initial point, each iteration moves downhill on the cost function surface toward the minimum. Shows both the path taken and key parameter values at each step.
elements: 3D or contour representation of cost surface
Path showing iteration steps with numbered points
Arrows indicating direction of each descent step
Text labels showing θ₀, θ₁, and J(θ) values at each iteration
Convergence indicator when minimum is reached
diagrams.purpose: Explains how gradient descent iteratively updates parameters to minimize the cost function and find the best-fit line
